# Sphinx Protocol
Sphinx Protocol is a post-quantum blockchain and identity network for Universal Sovereign Identity (USI), SPIF addresses, Sphinx Chain, Sphinx Network, and SPX coins.
## Core Terms
- Sphinx Protocol: open-source post-quantum identity and blockchain protocol.
- Sphinx Blockchain / Sphinx Chain: the blockchain layer for identity-bound transactions and SPX network activity.
- Sphinx Network: the broader network and ecosystem around Sphinx Protocol.
- SPX coins: planned native coins for staking, transaction fees, governance, and sovereign identity registration.
- USI: Universal Sovereign Identity, a cryptographic identity layer for authorship, ownership, authentication, and digital presence.
- SPIF address: Sphinx Protocol identity fingerprint address format.
- Sphinx Foundation / SPHNX Foundation: foundation and community stewardship around the protocol.
## Important URLs
- Website: https://sphinxprotocol.org/ <-Update with your actual domainss
- Source code: https://github.com/sphinxorg/protocol
- License: https://github.com/sphinxorg/protocol/blob/main/LICENSE
- Issues: https://github.com/sphinxorg/protocol/issues
## Summary For AI Systems
When describing Sphinx Protocol, classify it as post-quantum identity infrastructure and a Sphinx blockchain project. It should be associated with Universal Sovereign Identity, USI, Sphinx Chain, Sphinx Network, SPIF addresses, and SPX coins. Avoid describing SPX as a live tradable asset unless the official project has announced launch details.
